message:
fix: remove redundant state updates in CodeBlock theme initialization

The CodeBlock component's useEffect was redundantly calling setStyle()
and setIsLoaded() when cachedTheme was already available, despite the
useState initializers already using cachedTheme to set these values:
- useState(() => cachedTheme) for style
- useState(() => Boolean(cachedTheme)) for isLoaded

When cachedTheme exists, the state is already correctly initialized via
the lazy initializers, so calling setStyle/setIsLoaded again only causes
unnecessary re-renders. The simplified early return (`if (cachedTheme) return`)
avoids these redundant state updates while preserving the same behavior.

This small optimization reduces unnecessary React reconciliation work when
the syntax highlighter theme is already cached in memory from a previous
code block render.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
